headline: Nasdaq Moves a Step Closer to Tokenized Stocks. 24/7 Trading Is Coming Soon.
summary: Nasdaq  intends to launch a new design for tokenized equities, moving the world’s second-largest stock exchange one step closer to nonstop trading.  The exchange is working with Payward, the parent company of the global crypto platform Kraken, to design the tokenization infrastructure.  Tokenization refers to converting company shares or other equities into digital stock that can be traded on a decentralized blockchain ledger rather than through traditional exchanges.

headline: Bitcoin Prices Rebound From Oil-Fueled Retreat, Nasdaq To Launch Tokenized Stocks
summary: Bitcoin price rebounds from oil-fueled sell-off over the weekend. Nasdaq partners with Kraken to launch tokenized equities in 2027.

raw.headline: Bitcoin Prices Rebound From Oil-Fueled Retreat, Nasdaq To Launch Tokenized Stocks
raw.summary: Bitcoin price rebounds from oil-fueled sell-off over the weekend. Nasdaq partners with Kraken to launch tokenized equities in 2027.

headline: Nasdaq Plans Equity-Token Design, Partnering With Kraken Parent
summary: The initiative follows a tokenization proposal, filed with the US Securities and Exchange Commission in September, in which Nasdaq said it’s planning to enable equity securities to trade on its markets and settle in token form through the Depository Trust & Clearing Corp. Nasdaq said Monday that partner Payward, the parent company of global crypto platform Kraken, will design a gateway for tokenized equities to move between regulated markets and on-chain markets.  “Our partnership with Nasdaq helps build the liquidity layer and applications needed for tokenized equities to function within a global, always-on market structure,” Arjun Sethi, co-chief executive officer of Payward and Kraken, said in the statement.  Separately, Nasdaq announced a partnership with Seturion, Boerse Stuttgart Group’s pan-European settlement platform for tokenized assets.

headline: Stocks to Watch Monday: Moderna, Live Nation, Pfizer, Hims & Hers
summary: ↘️ Carnival (CCL), American Airlines (AAL) and United Airlines (UAL): Carriers and cruise operators are expected to face higher fuel costs due to the energy-price surge. Shares fell in premarket trading.

headline: Nasdaq Partners With Kraken in Plan for 24/7 Tokenized Stock Trading
summary: Nasdaq sought approval in September to let investors trade tokenized versions of its listed stocks and other exchange-traded products.

raw.headline: Nasdaq Partners With Kraken in Plan for 24/7 Tokenized Stock Trading
raw.summary: Nasdaq sought approval in September to let investors trade tokenized versions of its listed stocks and other exchange-traded products.
